items.title: Using fluorescently labeled wheat germ agglutinin to track lipopolysaccharide transport to the outer membrane in Escherichia coli
items.why: Introduces a direct live-cell readout for lipopolysaccharide trafficking into the outer membrane.

items.title: The mycomembrane
items.why: Summarizes key architectural principles of the mycomembrane and where mechanism-level gaps remain.

items.title: The conserved σD envelope stress response monitors multiple aspects of envelope integrity in corynebacteria
items.why: Shows how a conserved stress pathway surveils multiple envelope failure points in corynebacteria.

items.title: Co-ordinated assembly of the multilayered cell envelope of Gram-negative bacteria
items.why: Frames envelope assembly as a coordinated multilayer process relevant to antibiotic vulnerability.

items.title: FacZ is a GpsB-interacting protein that prevents aberrant division-site placement in Staphylococcus aureus
items.why: Identifies FacZ as a spatial regulator that prevents misplaced division events in Staphylococcus aureus.

items.title: PcdA recruitment during cytokinesis
items.why: Highlights division-stage localization behavior for the PcdA pathway during septal envelope remodeling.

items.title: Identification of FacZ as a division site placement factor in Staphylococcus aureus
items.why: Establishes FacZ as a new division-site placement factor and motivated subsequent mechanistic studies.

items.title: Cell wall synthesis and remodelling dynamics determine division site architecture and cell shape in Escherichia coli
items.why: Connects real-time synthesis and hydrolysis dynamics to division geometry and cell-shape control.
